strategy_notes: HIGH CASH DEFENSIVE PHASE — Universal MACD bearish persists across all 50 names. Core principles: (1) Took profits on AMZN (+12%) and GOOGL (+8.6%) — enforcing the +5-7% profit-taking rule I previously ignored. (2) Holding only NVDA as the single strongest relative strength name with a tight 4% trailing stop. (3) Cash target: ~80% — maximum defense in universal bearish MACD. (4) NO new buys until: at least 5 names show MACD bullish crossovers AND volume confirms (up days with expanding volume). (5) Volume divergence research reinforces caution: narrow AI-led rallies without broad participation are fragile. (6) If NVDA triggers stop, go 100% cash. (7) Watch for rotation signals: if breadth improves (10+ MACD crossovers), deploy cash aggressively into confirmed leaders. (8) Key watchlist for re-entry: META, AVGO, AMD (semiconductor momentum), V/MA (if rotation to quality cyclicals). (9) Realized gains compound; unrealized gains evaporate. Take profits in bearish technical regimes.

recent_trades.reason: Adding to NVDA position using pyramiding approach. NVDA +5.59% today, leading the semiconductor rally. AI/datacenter secular tailwind is intact. If the Iran de-escalation narrative holds, growth/cyclical tech will continue leading. This is a confirmation add — the initial entry was yesterday and it's already confirming with strong momentum. Scaling up per pyramiding research: add to winners showing confirmation.

Adding to META position. +6.67% today — strongest performer in the top 50. Mega-cap tech is leading this rotation with strong ad revenue fundamentals and AI investments. Pyramiding into a winner that's confirming. If this rally has legs, META will be among the biggest beneficiaries of a sentiment shift.

GOOGL +5.14% today — strong participation in the tech rally. Diversifying cyclical exposure across mega-cap tech rather than concentrating in just two names. GOOGL benefits from same AI/advertising tailwinds as META but adds search/cloud diversification. Initial position size per pyramiding strategy — start moderate, add on confirmation next cycle.

ABBV -0.94% today on a +1.06% SPY day — significant relative weakness. Defensive healthcare is clearly rotating out as the market shifts to cyclical/growth leadership. Still +2.41% from entry, so locking in a small win. This frees ~$12.5K for better-positioned names. Following the proven pattern: sell defensives showing negative relative strength on rally days (same signal that correctly flagged COST and XOM exits).

AMZN +2.11% today — participating strongly in the cyclical rally. Diversifies tech exposure beyond pure advertising (META/GOOGL) into e-commerce/cloud (AWS). AMZN benefits from consumer spending recovery if geopolitical resolution boosts confidence. Starting at 18% per pyramiding approach — moderate initial size, will add on confirmation next cycle. This fills the 5th position slot freed by ABBV sale.

AMZN is the weakest position at -1.38% P&L, down -0.38% today on a flat market day. Strategy notes explicitly flagged AMZN as 'first trim candidate if portfolio needs derisking.' Day 3 of the rally is showing no follow-through, and AMZN is underperforming all other holdings. Cutting the weakest name frees ~$18K to cash, reducing portfolio exposure from ~77% to ~59%. In MACD-unconfirmed environments, protecting capital is priority #1. Taking a small loss (-1.38%) is better than waiting for the -6% stop.

META is -0.82% today, the worst performer among my holdings. Down -0.65% from entry with MACD bearish. Research on sector rotation timing warns that mega-cap tech leadership is exhausting. META was flagged as 'next trim candidate if weakness continues' in my strategy notes. Taking a small loss (-0.65%) to raise cash to ~43%. Following the proven pattern: cut underperformers in MACD-bearish environments.

AMZN is +12.08% from entry — well past the +5% and even +7% profit-taking thresholds in my strategy. MACD still bearish across all 50 names. Strategy notes explicitly flagged AMZN as a trim candidate. Taking full profit here locks in ~$2,490 gain. In universal MACD-bearish environments, realized gains > unrealized gains. This raises cash to ~60% which is appropriate for current conditions.

GOOGL is +8.64% from entry, well past the +5% profit-taking threshold. GOOGL was -0.39% today while semis rallied hard — showing relative weakness within tech. MACD bearish. Strategy notes flagged GOOGL as next trim candidate after AMZN. Locking in ~$1,738 gain. With universal bearish MACD and narrow AI-only leadership, reducing to single strongest position is the disciplined move.

last_decision.market_assessment: SPY at $679.46 is flat (-0.07%), with the market posting a second straight winning week amid a fragile US-Iran ceasefire narrative. However, ALL 50 names still show bearish MACD signals — this is the strongest universal caution signal possible. Notably, semiconductor/AI names (NVDA +2.57%, AMD +3.55%, AVGO +4.69%) are leading while defensives and cyclicals are broadly weak (COST -3.25%, CRM -3.45%, ACN -3.49%, NKE -3.14%). This is a narrow, AI-driven rally without broad participation — a classic sign of fragile leadership that can reverse sharply.
last_decision.self_reflection: My defensive cash-raise strategy from the last cycle has proven correct so far — cutting META at -0.65% was smart as it's now at $629 (would have been a bigger gain, but the MACD-bearish discipline is the right framework). My 3 remaining positions (AMZN +12.08%, GOOGL +8.64%, NVDA +7.71%) are all performing well despite universal bearish MACD. The key lesson: in MACD-bearish environments, holding winners that confirm with momentum is fine, but profit-taking discipline at the 5-7% threshold should have been applied — all three positions have now blown past the +5% profit-taking rule I set. I'm sitting on unrealized gains that I should be protecting. The research on volume-price divergence confirms my concern: if these up-moves are on declining volume, the rally is fragile. I need to enforce my profit-taking rules more strictly rather than letting winners run unchecked in bearish technical regimes.

This raises cash to ~60% which is appropriate for current conditions.

Strategy notes flagged GOOGL as next trim candidate after AMZN. Locking in ~$1,738 gain. NVDA is +7.71% from entry and was the strongest performer today (+2.57%) in the semiconductor rally. It's the last position I flagged to trim and the clear relative strength leader. However, I'm tightening the stop-loss to 4% from current levels ($181.08). If NVDA breaks below $181, I exit. This keeps ~20.5% of portfolio invested in the single strongest name while raising cash to ~80%. NVDA is the AI secular leader and benefits most from any continuation of this narrow rally.

last_decision.next_research: Re-entry timing after extended cash positions — specifically how to identify the optimal moment to redeploy cash when transitioning from universal bearish MACD to early bullish signals, including the sequencing of which sectors typically lead recoveries, false-start frequency, and whether buying the first MACD crossover or waiting for 3-5 confirmations produces better risk-adjusted returns
